set.sourceBehavior: The manifest binds the Foodike application label, launcher icon, splash theme, backup/xml resource references, and launcher activity metadata to Android startup resources.
set.targetBehavior: `AppScope/app.json5`, `entry/src/main/module.json5`, and the target color/media/string resources preserve the app identity, launcher metadata, start-window background/icon, and page profile wiring for the HarmonyOS entry module.
set.notes: Mapped manifest resource/configuration wiring into HarmonyOS app and module metadata instead of Android manifest attributes.

set.sourceBehavior: The manifest defines a single launcher entry point through MAIN/LAUNCHER intent-filter for `MainActivity`; no deeplink, URI pattern, or extra bundle contract is declared here.
set.targetBehavior: `entry/src/main/module.json5` exposes the single HarmonyOS home entry ability with `action.system.home` and `entity.system.home`, preserving the source app's single-launch entry contract without adding deeplink behavior.
set.notes: Closed narrowly to launcher-entry semantics only; source manifest does not declare custom deeplinks.

verificationEvidence: Source inspection: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml contains only android.intent.action.MAIN and android.intent.category.LAUNCHER for MainActivity.

set.sourceBehavior: The analyzer flagged runtime permissions from manifest context, but the inspected manifest declares no dangerous permissions and no protected API access contract.
set.targetBehavior: No HarmonyOS permission wiring was added because the source manifest contains no permission declarations or permission result flow to preserve.
set.notes: Closed from direct source inspection as a manifest-level false positive.

verificationEvidence: Source inspection: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml contains no <uses-permission> entries and no permission-related activity/provider/service declarations.

set.sourceBehavior: The manifest declares an `Application` subclass plus a single launcher `Activity`, establishing the app-level component lifecycle and entry shell for startup.
set.targetBehavior: HarmonyOS replaces the Android application/activity pair with `FoodikeAbilityStage` and `EntryAbility`, keeping a thin lifecycle shell that owns startup logging, preference-backed bootstrap inspection, and window content loading.
set.notes: Platform replacement from Android Application/Activity lifecycle to HarmonyOS AbilityStage/UIAbility lifecycle.

verificationEvidence: Source inspection: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml declares application .FoodikeApp and launcher activity .presentation.MainActivity.

set.sourceBehavior: The launcher activity starts under `Theme.SplashScreenTheme`, then hands control to the main Compose shell during activity creation.
set.targetBehavior: `EntryAbility.onCreate` and `EntryAbility.onWindowStageCreate` perform the HarmonyOS startup handoff: inspect persisted login preference state during ability creation and load the bootstrap page into the main `WindowStage`.
set.notes: This is startup shell parity only; full routed startup gating remains planned for phase 2.

verificationEvidence: Source inspection: MainActivity installs splash screen and sets content in onCreate; manifest/theme wire startup resources.
Target inspection: app_ability.cj reads persisted login flag from HarmonyOS Preferences and windowStage.loadContent("FoodikeBootstrap").

set.sourceBehavior: `app/build.gradle.kts` brings in Navigation Compose and bottom-navigation related UI dependencies that back the source route set and tab shell.
set.targetBehavior: `app_router.cj` defines the full source route identifiers and stable route-path mapping as the HarmonyOS navigation foundation, but phase 1 stops short of rendering the full shell UI until later phases.
set.notes: Simplified to route contract foundation in phase 1; visible navigation shell remains phase-02 work.

set.sourceBehavior: The app module build config enables Compose and the Android splash dependency used by `MainActivity` to hold startup until the initial route is resolved.
set.targetBehavior: Phase 1 preserves the startup foundation with `EntryAbility` lifecycle hooks, main-window loading, and direct inspection of the persisted login flag, but does not yet render the final onboarding/home route split before page composition.
set.notes: Simplified startup lifecycle parity; full user-visible route gating is deferred to phase 2 per plan.
